ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-11-2024 Continued To Mar-19-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Jan-10-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for default judgment prove up against Defendant Francisco Leon, an individual aka Reyes Francisco Leon Rodriguez aka Leon Francisco and Defendant Leon Barranco Multi-Services a corporation.

Dept: 505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Bailiff/s: Deputy Wong and Nguyen; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:09:44 a.m., pausing at 10:11:20 a.m., resuming at 10:26:52 a.m. and concluding at 10:54:53 a.m.(D505)

George R. Hynick, Esq. appearing for and on behalf of Plaintiff Legend Advance Funding II, LLC, A limited liability company via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Francisco Leon, an individual aka Reyes Francisco Leon Rodriguez aka Leon Francisco not appearing.
Defendant Leon Barranco Multi-Services a corporation not appearing.
Lisa Adjoda, witness for the Plaintiff, was placed under oath via Zoom Video Conference.

Plaintiff's complaint for Breach of Contract, breach of guaranty filed June 5, 2019
Proof of substituted service as to Defendant named Doe 1: Leon Barranco Multi-Services, a corporation; served July 15, 2022; mailed July 19, 2022; service effectuated on July 28, 2022.
Proof of substituted service as to Defendant Francisco Leon, an individual aka Reyes Francisco Leon Rodriguez aka Leon Francisco; served July 15, 2022; mailed July 18, 2022; service effectuated on July 27, 2022.
Default entered as to Defendant Leon Barranco Multi-Services, a corporation on March 13, 2023.
Default entered as to Defendant Francisco Leon, an individual aka Reyes Francisco Leon Rodriguez aka Leon Francisco on March 13, 2023.
Defendant Does 2-100 dismissed on June 27, 2023.
Defendant Francisco Leon an individual dba Leon Barranco Multi Service dismissed on September 28, 2023.

Counsel to recite a brief summary of the case, to clarify the names of defaulted defendants and to provide detailed proof as to what the witness will attest to.

Plaintiff submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit A: Merchant sales agreement
Exhibit B: Merchant Security agreement and guaranty

Court examines the witness.

Witness, as collections paralegal and custodian of records, confirm that declaration made are true and correct.

Court refers to the prayer for judgment. Court reviews amounts requested.

The matter of interest and attorney's fees and costs is discussed.

Based on the declarations and testimony, the plaintiff has proven their prima facie case and Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defendants as follows:

Plaintiff Legend Advance Funding II, LLC, A limited liability company recover from Defendant Francisco Leon, an individual aka Reyes Francisco Leon Rodriguez aka Leon Francisco and Defendant Leon Barranco Multi-Services a corporation:

Damages $25,796.83
Attorney's Fees $7,000.00
Costs $599.00

Total $33,395.83

Proposed judgment to be submitted for review and signature of the Court.
